title: Confine a file reader to its base dir against traversal and sibling-prefix escape

metadata.mainstream_gap: The natural fix (normpath + startswith(BASE)) blocks ../ but still leaks a sibling directory whose name has BASE as a prefix (public vs publicus); the only correct confinement uses realpath plus an os.sep boundary or commonpath.

input.prompt:
safe_read.py exposes read_asset(user_path), which is supposed to read files ONLY from the bundled public/ directory next to this module. As written it does no confinement at all, so callers can read arbitrary files via path traversal (e.g. ../secret.txt) or absolute paths. Fix read_asset so that:
- Legitimate nested reads inside public/ still work (e.g. "img/logo.txt" and "./img/logo.txt").
- Any path that resolves OUTSIDE public/ raises ValueError. This includes ../ traversal, absolute paths, AND a sibling directory whose name merely starts with the base name (e.g. a directory named publicus next to public must NOT be readable via ../publicus/...).
Keep the function name read_asset and its single str parameter. Do not read from any location other than the confined base directory.

expectations.test:
import os
base = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
pub = os.path.join(base, "public")
os.makedirs(os.path.join(pub, "img"), exist_ok=True)
with open(os.path.join(pub, "img", "logo.txt"), "w") as f:
    f.write("LOGO-OK")
with open(os.path.join(base, "secret.txt"), "w") as f:
    f.write("TOP-SECRET")
sib = os.path.join(base, "publicus")
os.makedirs(sib, exist_ok=True)
with open(os.path.join(sib, "leak.txt"), "w") as f:
    f.write("SIBLING-LEAK")
from safe_read import read_asset
# legitimate nested reads still work
assert read_asset("img/logo.txt") == "LOGO-OK"
assert read_asset("./img/logo.txt") == "LOGO-OK"
# classic traversal and sibling-prefix escape are blocked
for evil in ["../secret.txt", "../../secret.txt", "img/../../secret.txt", "img/../../publicus/leak.txt", "../publicus/leak.txt"]:
    try:
        read_asset(evil)
        raise AssertionError("escape not blocked: " + evil)
    except ValueError:
        pass
# absolute-path escape is blocked
try:
    read_asset(os.path.join(base, "secret.txt"))
    raise AssertionError("absolute escape not blocked")
except ValueError:
    pass

title: Eliminate command injection by moving from shell=True string to an argv list

metadata.mainstream_gap: Tempting partial fixes either keep shell=True (so injection or quoting bugs persist) or pass an argv list WITH shell=True, which on POSIX silently drops the file argument (shell runs argv[0] only) and breaks legitimate use; only an argv list with shell=False both blocks injection and preserves behavior.

input.prompt: archiver.py builds a shell command by string-concatenating an untrusted filename and runs it with shell=True, which is a command-injection hole: a filename like 'notes.txt; rm -rf ~' would execute arbitrary commands. Fix count_lines so that the untrusted filename can never be interpreted by a shell, while the function still returns the correct integer line count of a real file. Keep the function name count_lines and its single str parameter. It must still invoke the system `wc -l` tool to count lines (do not reimplement counting in pure Python).

expectations.test:
import os
from archiver import count_lines
base = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
legit = os.path.join(base, "notes.txt")
with open(legit, "w") as f:
    f.write("a\nb\nc\n")
# legitimate use still returns the correct count
assert count_lines(legit) == 3, "legit count failed"
# injection via ';' must not execute a second command
marker = os.path.join(base, "PWNED")
if os.path.exists(marker):
    os.remove(marker)
try:
    count_lines("notes.txt; touch " + marker)
except Exception:
    pass
assert not os.path.exists(marker), "command injection executed via ;"
# injection via command substitution must not execute either
marker2 = os.path.join(base, "PWNED2")
if os.path.exists(marker2):
    os.remove(marker2)
try:
    count_lines("$(touch " + marker2 + ")")
except Exception:
    pass
assert not os.path.exists(marker2), "command injection executed via $()"

title: Fix shared mutable default argument that accumulates across calls

metadata.mainstream_gap: The default list is created once at def time; calls without the arg share state. Fix must keep the signature shape.

input.prompt: collect.py: collect(item, bucket=[]) wrongly shares one list across calls that omit bucket. Fix so each call without an explicit bucket starts empty, while an explicit bucket is still used. Keep the function name collect. Print DONE.

expectations.test:
assert collect(1)==[1]
assert collect(2)==[2]
b=[]
assert collect(9,b)==[9]
assert b==[9]
